one_liner: GOOG at $356 sits between both EMAs with 15% DCF upside, Grade A fundamentals, and earnings July 22 — the setup is clean pre-earnings with RSI neutral at 51 and volume drying.

price_action.intraday_story: GOOG recovered from an early low of $348.66 to close near the session high at $356.24, finishing above both EMAs (EMA50 $354.60, EMA200 $353.75). The stock sandwiched perfectly between EMAs on low volume. Constructive pre-earnings base.

news_catalyst.headline: Earnings July 22 — AI Monetization and Search Dominance Under the Microscope
news_catalyst.summary: No Alphabet-specific news today. The July 22 earnings will be a critical test — Alphabet recently raised $85B in equity suggesting massive AI capex pressure. The market will focus on Google Search revenue trends, Gemini AI adoption, YouTube advertising, and Google Cloud growth. The 15% DCF upside at $408 is modest but supported by Grade A fundamentals.

technical.summary: GOOG's close above both EMAs ($354.60 and $353.75) on declining volume is a consolidation pattern consistent with pre-earnings positioning. RSI at 51 is perfectly neutral. The upper BB at $374.23 is the near-term resistance target. Wyckoff support at $331.35 is the risk level. Consolidation above dual-EMA support ahead of July 22 earnings — no distribution pressure visible.

wyckoff_narrative: Consolidation above dual-EMA support ahead of July 22 earnings. Volume drying is the key constructive signal — no distribution pressure visible. A pre-earnings accumulate with a stop below $348 (today's low) is a reasonable setup. The $331-337 Wyckoff support zone is the invalidation level.

valuation.summary: DCF fair value $408 vs $356 market price — 14.5% upside. Grade A fundamentals support the modest margin of safety. Reasonable but not deeply discounted — the accumulate case rests on earnings execution.

key_strengths: Search monopoly with AI integration preserving dominance
YouTube and Cloud growing in double digits
key_concerns: Raised $85B in equity — massive AI capex pressure on FCF
July 22 earnings: any AI threat to Search revenue would be a severe negative
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with the AI capex overhang as the key risk. July 22 earnings will define the next leg.

risks: Close below both EMAs (EMA200 $353.75) on volume would abort pre-earnings setup
July 22 earnings: Search revenue deceleration from AI competition would be severely negative
